subtasks.description: Extract Microsoft licensed user counts and SKU details from SAM (Software Asset Management) system
Query consumption facts database to retrieve active user counts and actual usage patterns across Microsoft products
Cross-reconcile SAM licensed user counts against consumption facts active users, calculating variance by product/SKU
Analyze reconciliation results to identify under-provisioned (more active users than licensed) and over-provisioned (more licenses than active users) scenarios with financial impact
Generate policy and procurement actions to optimize licensing spend based on identified gaps (e.g., license adjustments, true-up recommendations, reallocation strategies)

plan.cost_rationale: Mid-tier specialist agents appropriate for data extraction, complex reconciliation logic, and policy recommendations. No frontier-tier complexity required as this follows standard SAM reconciliation workflow.

output.notes: No upstream SAM document, file, or structured data was provided in this task. All fields are null. Please supply the SAM export (CSV, XML, JSON, or report PDF) containing Microsoft SKU names, assigned user counts, active user counts, and cost data to enable extraction and reconciliation.

reconciled.notes: MongoDB collection 'sam_licenses' returned no documents and SAM extract contains no structured data. No SKU-level licensed user counts or active user consumption facts are available to reconcile. Variance calculation and under/over-provisioning classification cannot be performed.
reconciled.recommended_action: Supply SAM export (CSV, XML, JSON, or PDF) containing Microsoft SKU names, assigned user counts, active user counts, and cost data, and ensure the 'sam_licenses' MongoDB collection is populated with consumption facts before re-running this reconciliation task.

output.reason: The task involves generating policy and procurement actions derived from upstream license gap analysis (SAM vs consumption facts). IAM permission check is confirmed upstream (iam.check_permission on sam_licenses). The action is permitted with constraints: financial recommendations above defined thresholds require procurement/finance approval; any reallocation or true-up actions affecting vendor contracts require vendor management review; PII exposure (tenant/user data) must be anonymized in output reports; and all generated procurement actions must be logged to the audit trail before execution. POL-CHANGE-007 is flagged pending_review because license true-up recommendations may constitute contractual changes with Microsoft requiring authorized signatory approval.
applied_constraints: Financial recommendations exceeding $10,000 USD in annualized impact must be routed to Finance and Procurement for dual approval before actioning
License reallocation strategies must not reduce assigned_user_count below active_user_count peak thresholds observed in the last 90-day consumption window
True-up recommendations involving Microsoft SKU contract amendments require Vendor Management and Legal review prior to submission
All tenant_id and tenant_name fields in generated output must be scoped to authorized viewers only; no PII or tenant identifiers to be included in cross-tenant aggregate reports
Generated procurement actions must be written to the audit log with originating upstream task ID, timestamp, and acting agent identity before any execution
Reallocation strategies must include a 30-day buffer/grace period recommendation to account for usage spikes before license count reductions are finalized
Any identified under-provisioning shortfalls flagged as estimated_shortfall_usd must trigger an alert to the license owner before automated procurement action is taken
Output actions are advisory only; no automated contract modifications or purchase orders may be issued without human-in-the-loop approval from an authorized procurement officer
